page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The system is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside weaker multi-core scaling. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as average at 1841.49, whereas the multi-core CPU performance falls into the weak tier at 3867.76. Memory bandwidth tests show a weak read-only performance of 208.37 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 30.43 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth remains average at 25.42 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, perform at average levels, but multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor at 20.78 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput is average at 6.35 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading, which ensures consistent compute performance for workloads that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios. However, the lack of local storage and the weak multi-core scaling relative to its 4 physical cores represent notable tradeoffs. It is best suited for lightweight compute-bound tasks, single-threaded applications, and basic web serving where predictable CPU performance is required but memory and storage demands are minimal.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 dedicated physical cores from an Intel Xeon 8369B processor clocked at 2.7 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance (1841.49 in stress-ng) and average static web serving throughput (6.35 GB/sec), though multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated, single-threaded, or moderately compute-bound workloads. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic application hosting, and single-threaded processing tasks that do not require high memory density or local storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.xlarge is a KVM-based compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized virtual machine featuring four physical Intel Xeon cores and a balanced profile for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated physical Intel Xeon 8369B cores at 2.7 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average single-core CPU and static web serving performance
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded processing
